# BI-260005: Align workflow and release operations with section-7 deployment model

- Backlog Item ID: BI-260005
- Title: Finalize build/deploy operation model for GitHub Pages release
- Status: Ready
- Persona/Actor: Repository owner or release manager
- Value statement: For a release manager, define a clear, automated build and deployment process to
  ensure reliable and repeatable releases to GitHub Pages.
- Context: Section 7 deployment decisions are accepted and need to be enforced consistently across
  workflow configuration and release checks.
- Problem statement: Without a locked release model, deployment behavior and rollback may become
  inconsistent.
- Expected outcome: A repeatable deploy pipeline with clear config ownership and rollback steps.
- Success metric(s): Main-branch changes deploy consistently; rollback can be executed by revert.
- Acceptance criteria (behavior):
  - Workflow deploys on `main` push and supports manual dispatch.
  - Build/deploy chain produces and publishes `dist`.
  - Build-time app URL and base-path values are explicitly configured.
  - Release verification includes landing route, legal/add-on routes, and app-sign-in link check.
  - Rollback is documented and tested via revert-and-redeploy approach.
- Non-goals: Introducing separate runtime infrastructure for the static public site.
- Constraints: GitHub Pages static deployment model.
- Dependencies: DNS/domain ownership and final public URL confirmation in repository settings.
- Risks/assumptions: Canonical URL and Pages settings must stay synchronized.
- Validation approach: CI run review plus manual smoke verification after deploy.
- Priority score: High
- Target window: Before first production launch
- Links:
  - Requirement entry: REQ-FR-260005.md
  - ADR: ADR-260005.md
  - ICR: n/a
  - PR/changeset: n/a
